'''Shaun Lunt''' is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

Lunt played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in [[RFL Championship#2002–2008: National Leagues|National League One]], [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League 1|National League Two]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He played on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the Super League.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Lunt played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and on loan from [[Hull KR]] at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Lunt was born in [[Cockermouth]], [[Cumbria, England]].

==Career==
===Castleford Tigers===
Lunt started his professional career at [[Castleford Tigers]]

===Workington Town===
Lunt then moved on to play for [[Workington Town]].
[[File:Shaun Lunt.JPG|thumb|right|Lunt playing for Huddersfield in 2009]]

===Huddersfield Giants===
Lunt joined Huddersfield in time for the 2009 Super League season. Lunt has made a promising start to life in Super League, scoring a try on his début, and adding four more in the first half of the season. On 10 May 2009, Lunt scored his first career hat-trick for Huddersfield in the [[Challenge Cup]] fourth round against [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]] in a 38-12 victory.  He played in Huddersfield's [[2009 Challenge Cup Final]] loss, scoring a try and having one disallowed in the process.

===Leeds Rhinos===
In 2012 he joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on a one-year loan.  Lunt played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] side at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>
Lunt played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>

===Huddersfield Giants===
Lunt returned from his loan spell with the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club, and once again played for the [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] side.
[[File:Shaun Lunt Hull KR.jpg|thumb|upright|Lunt playing for Hull KR]]

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Lunt went on loan to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2015 and signed for them in 2016.  On 6 January 2017, it was revealed that Lunt would be captain of Hull Kingston Rovers for the 2017 season.  On 26 June 2019, Lunt re-joined the Leeds club on a deal until the end of the season, swapping with [[Matt Parcell]] who joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 21 November 2019 it was announced that Lunt would join Batley for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/shaun-lunt-joins-batley-for-2020/|title=Shaun Lunt joins Batley for 2020|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=9 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
It was announced on 12 August 2020 that Lunt would leave Batley to join Barrow for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.batleynews.co.uk/sport/other-sport/shaun-lunt-leave-batley-bulldogs-barrow-raiders-ahead-2021-campaign-2939689|title=Shaun Lunt to leave Batley Bulldogs for Barrow Raiders ahead of 2021 campaign|publisher=Batley & Birstall News|accessdate=9 Dec 2020}}</ref>

Without playing a game for new club Barrow, Lunt announced his retirement from rugby league on 27 Feb 2021<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Feb 2021|accessdate=1 Mar 2021|title=Shaun Lunt announces retirement|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/shaun-lunt-announces-retirement/}}</ref>

==International career==
Lunt represented England in 2010, and he played for the England Knights in 2012.

==Style of play==
Lunt was known for his great speed out of [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|dummy half]] and accurate distribution, along with his sharp eye for the try-line, which led to many tries from dummy half in his career.

==Honours==
===Club===
[[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]
* [[Challenge Cup]] (1): 2009 Runner Up
* [[League Leaders' Shield]] (1): [[Super League XVIII|2013]]

[[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]
* [[Super League]] (1): [[Super League XVII|2012]]
* [[Challenge Cup]] (1): 2012 Runner Up

[[Hull Kingston Rovers]]
* [[Challenge Cup]] (1): 2015 Runner Up
